risks: ML online endpoint uses Key-based authentication instead of managed identity, increasing secret management burden and reducing security posture
No network isolation or private endpoints configured for any resources, leaving all services publicly accessible
Storage account uses Standard_LRS with no geo-redundancy; model artifacts could be lost in a datacenter failure
Azure Container Registry is not explicitly included in the plan; ML workspace auto-creates one but without custom security configuration
No resource tagging strategy defined for cost tracking, environment identification, or operational governance
Application Insights uses classic (non-workspace-based) deployment; Microsoft recommends workspace-based App Insights for new resources
No explicit autoscale rules defined for the ML online endpoint; high-volume traffic spikes may cause latency without scaling configuration
correctionsRecommended: Switch ML online endpoint authMode from 'Key' to 'AADToken' and configure a managed identity for the workspace and endpoint
Add an Azure Container Registry resource with admin access disabled and anonymous pull disabled for ML image hosting
Consider upgrading storage redundancy to Standard_GRS or Standard_ZRS for production model artifact durability
Add a Log Analytics Workspace resource and configure Application Insights as workspace-based for unified log analytics
Define explicit autoscale settings (instance_count, scale_settings) on the online endpoint to handle high request volume spikes
Add resource tags (environment, project, cost-center) across all resources for governance and cost management
Consider adding a VNet and private endpoints for the ML workspace, storage, Key Vault, and Cosmos DB for network isolation
